Package org.solenopsis.soap.partner

Class DescribeLayout2

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.partner.DescribeLayout2
public class DescribeLayout2 extends Object

Java class for DescribeLayout complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="DescribeLayout">
   <complexContent>
     <restriction base="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}anyType">
       <sequence>
         <element name="buttonLayoutSection" type="{urn:partner.soap.sforce.com}DescribeLayoutButtonSection" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="detailLayoutSections" type="{urn:partner.soap.sforce.com}DescribeLayoutSection" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="editLayoutSections" type="{urn:partner.soap.sforce.com}DescribeLayoutSection" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="feedView" type="{urn:partner.soap.sforce.com}DescribeLayoutFeedView" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="highlightsPanelLayoutSection" type="{urn:partner.soap.sforce.com}DescribeLayoutSection" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="id" type="{urn:partner.soap.sforce.com}ID"/>
         <element name="quickActionList" type="{urn:partner.soap.sforce.com}DescribeQuickActionListResult" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="relatedContent" type="{urn:partner.soap.sforce.com}RelatedContent" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="relatedLists" type="{urn:partner.soap.sforce.com}RelatedList" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="saveOptions" type="{urn:partner.soap.sforce.com}DescribeLayoutSaveOption" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
       </sequence>
     </restriction>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

      public DescribeLayoutButtonSection getButtonLayoutSection()

      public void setButtonLayoutSection(DescribeLayoutButtonSection value)

      Parameters:
      value - allowed object is DescribeLayoutButtonSection

    • getDetailLayoutSections

      public List<DescribeLayoutSection> getDetailLayoutSections()
      Gets the value of the detailLayoutSections property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the detailLayoutSections property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getDetailLayoutSections().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list DescribeLayoutSection

      Returns:
      The value of the detailLayoutSections property.

    • getEditLayoutSections

      public List<DescribeLayoutSection> getEditLayoutSections()
      Gets the value of the editLayoutSections property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the editLayoutSections property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getEditLayoutSections().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list DescribeLayoutSection

      Returns:
      The value of the editLayoutSections property.

    • getRelatedLists

      public List<RelatedList> getRelatedLists()
      Gets the value of the relatedLists property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the relatedLists property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getRelatedLists().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list RelatedList

      Returns:
      The value of the relatedLists property.

    • getSaveOptions

      public List<DescribeLayoutSaveOption> getSaveOptions()
      Gets the value of the saveOptions property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the saveOptions property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getSaveOptions().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list DescribeLayoutSaveOption

      Returns:
      The value of the saveOptions property.
